title: Adjust Quantities by Fractional Transfer
description: When a fraction of one group is moved to another, adjust both quantities accordingly.
condition: A fraction f of quantity B is moved to quantity A.
response: Decrease B by fB and increase A by fB, then form the new ratio.
label: Principle / Model
example: If the ratio is $3:6$ and $\frac{1}{6}$ of box B (1 unit) moves to A, new amounts are $4:5$.

title: Calculate Percent of a Quantity
description: Find a specified percent of a given whole number.
condition: Given a whole number $W$ and a percent $p\%$.
response: Compute $(p/100) \times W$.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compare Computed Part Values
description: Compare two calculated part amounts to determine which is greater.
condition: Given two numerical part amounts derived from percent calculations.
response: Identify which quantity is larger and state the comparison (e.g., A is greater than B).
label: Production / Skill
example: Yuna plants $12$ flowers and Lisa plants $22$ flowers. Since $22 > 12$, Lisa plants more flowers than Yuna.

title: Compare Ratios Using Cross Products
description: Determine which of two ratios is greater by cross-multiplying and comparing products.
condition: Two ratios a:b and c:d are given and need to be compared.
response: Compute a·d and b·c and compare the products to determine which ratio is greater.
label: Principle / Model
example: Compare $\frac{4}{3}$ and $\frac{3}{2}$ by cross-multiplying: $4 \cdot 2 = 8$ and $3 \cdot 3 = 9$. Since $8 < 9$, $\frac{4}{3} < \frac{3}{2}$.

title: Compare Unit Rates
description: Determine which of two situations has the greater or smaller rate by computing and comparing unit rates.
condition: Given two ratios or rates describing different situations with different quantities and units.
response: Compute each unit rate (quantity per 1 unit) and compare their numerical values to determine which is greater or smaller.
label: Principle
example: Zebra: $15$ miles in $30$ minutes $= \frac{15}{30}=0.5$ miles/min. Horse: $20$ miles in $35$ minutes $\approx0.57$ miles/min. Since $0.5<0.57$, the zebra runs slower.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Compute Part from Ratio and Total
description: Given a part-to-part ratio and a total quantity, determine the quantity of one part.
condition: A ratio a:b and a total quantity T for the combined parts are given; find the quantity corresponding to one part of the ratio.
response: Compute total ratio units a+b, find unit value T/(a+b), then multiply by the relevant part (e.g., a or b).
label: Principle / Model
example: If the ratio of crayons to pencils is 7:5 and the total is 72, then total units = 7+5=12. Each unit is $72/12=6$. Crayons = $7 \times 6 = 42$.

title: Compute Total from Parts
description: Add multiple quantities to find a total amount.
condition: Given separate category counts and asked for the total.
response: Add the quantities to compute the total.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compute Unit Rate by Division
description: Find a unit rate by dividing the total quantity by the number of units.
condition: Given a ratio $a:b$ or a statement like "$a$ items in $b$ units of time" and asked for the amount per 1 unit.
response: Divide $a \div b$ to determine the amount per 1 unit.
label: Principle
example: $8$ lawns in $4$ days: $8 \div 4 = 2$ lawns per day.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Convert Miles per Minute to Feet per Second
description: Convert a rate from miles per minute to feet per second using unit conversion factors.
condition: Given a rate in miles per minute and asked for feet per second.
response: Multiply by $\frac{5280\text{ ft}}{1\text{ mi}}$ and divide by $60$ seconds per minute.
label: Principle / Model
example: $0.5\ \text{mi/min} \times \frac{5280}{1} \div 60 = 44\ \text{ft/s}$.

title: Convert Percent to Fraction
description: Rewrite a percent as a fraction with denominator 100 and simplify if possible.
condition: Given a percent value $p\%$.
response: Write $\frac{p}{100}$ and simplify if appropriate.
label: Principle / Model

title: Convert Units in a Rate
description: Convert a rate to different units by applying unit conversion factors.
condition: Given a rate expressed in one set of units (e.g., miles per minute) and asked for the rate in different units (e.g., feet per second).
response: Multiply by appropriate conversion factors to express the rate in the requested units.
label: Principle
example: $0.5$ miles/min $\times \frac{5280\text{ ft}}{1\text{ mile}} \times \frac{1\text{ min}}{60\text{ s}} = 44$ ft/s.

title: Convert Yards per Minute to Feet per Hour
description: Convert a rate from yards per minute to feet per hour using unit conversion factors.
condition: Given a rate in yards per minute and asked for feet per hour.
response: Multiply by $3$ (feet per yard) and by $60$ (minutes per hour).
label: Principle / Model
example: $80$ yd/min $\times 3 \times 60 = 14{,}400$ ft/hr.

title: Find Percent from Part and Whole
description: Determine the percent represented by a part of a whole.
condition: Given a part value and a whole value.
response: Compute $\frac{\text{part}}{\text{whole}} \times 100\%$.
label: Principle / Model

title: Find Whole from Percent and Part
description: Determine the total (whole) when given a part and its percent of the whole.
condition: Given that a number $a$ represents $p\%$ of a total.
response: Compute $a \div (p/100)$ to find the whole.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $21$ is $30\%$ of the total, then total $= 21 \div 0.30 = 70$.

title: Form Ratio from Two Quantities
description: Construct a ratio comparing two specified quantities in the stated order.
condition: Two quantities A and B are identified and a comparison "A to B" is requested.
response: Write the ratio as A:B in the given order.
label: Rule / Plan
example: If there are 9 plates and 6 bowls, the ratio of plates to bowls is $9:6$.

title: Generate Equivalent Ratios by Scaling
description: Create an equivalent ratio by multiplying or dividing both terms by the same nonzero number.
condition: Given a ratio $a:b$ and a need to match one term to a known quantity.
response: Multiply or divide both $a$ and $b$ by the same factor to produce an equivalent ratio.
label: Principle
example: $3:5$ scaled by $6$ gives $18:30$.

title: Identify Equivalent Ratios in a Table
description: Recognize that pairs of values in a ratio table are equivalent if one is a constant multiple of the other.
condition: A table lists paired quantities representing a proportional relationship.
response: Check that each pair maintains the same multiplicative relationship (same constant of proportionality).
label: Principle / Model
example: If $12:3$, then multiplying both by 3 gives $36:9$, preserving the ratio.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Identify Part and Whole in Context
description: Distinguish which quantity represents the whole and which represents the part in a percent situation.
condition: Given a word problem describing a percent relationship between two quantities.
response: Label the total quantity as the whole and the percent portion as the part.
label: Rule / Plan
example: In '90 is 25% of 360', $360$ is the whole and $90$ is the part.

title: Interpret Proportional Relationship from Graph
description: Determine whether plotted points represent the same ratio by checking for a common constant of proportionality.
condition: Two sets of plotted (x, y) points are shown and the relationship must be compared.
response: Determine whether each set forms a line through the origin with the same slope (same y/x ratio).
label: Principle / Model
example: If mixture 1 has points like $(4,8)$ and $(6,12)$ (ratio 2), and mixture 2 has $(3,6)$ (ratio 2), both share constant ratio 2.

title: Interpret Ratio as Multiplicative Relationship
description: Translate a ratio into a multiplicative comparison between two quantities.
condition: A ratio a:b relates two quantities (e.g., small rocks to large rocks).
response: Express one quantity as a multiple of the other (e.g., small = 3 × large if 21:7).
label: Principle / Model
example: Since $21:7 = 3:1$, the number of small rocks is $3 \times$ the number of large rocks.

title: Interpret Ratio in Context
description: Explain what each term in a ratio represents within a given situation.
condition: Given a ratio $a:b$ in a word problem context.
response: State what $a$ represents and what $b$ represents in terms of the context.
label: Rule
example: In $5:14$, $5$ represents blue shirts and $14$ represents total shirts.

title: Interpret Ratio Language in Context
description: Correctly match the order of quantities in a verbal ratio statement to numerical form.
condition: A statement such as 'red to blue' or 'tablespoons to cups' is given.
response: Write the ratio in the stated order (first quantity : second quantity).
label: Rule / Plan
example: 'Red to blue is 2 to 1' means $\frac{\text{red}}{\text{blue}} = 2:1$.

title: Interpret Verbal Ratio Statement
description: Translate a verbal description of a ratio into the correct pair of quantities.
condition: A phrase of the form "the ratio of X to Y" is given.
response: Identify quantity X as the first term and quantity Y as the second term of the ratio.
label: Concept
example: "The ratio of mugs to plates" means mugs:plates (e.g., $1:9$).

title: Multiply Unit Rate by Quantity
description: Find total amount by multiplying a unit rate by the number of units.
condition: Given a unit rate $r$ per 1 unit and a quantity $n$ units.
response: Compute $r \times n$.
label: Principle / Model
example: If rate is $0.3$ and total is $270$, then $0.3 \times 270 = 81$.

title: Represent Percent on a 100-Grid
description: Shade the correct number of squares on a 10×10 grid to model a percent.
condition: Given a percent value and a 100-square grid.
response: Shade the number of squares equal to the percent value.
label: Production / Skill
example: To represent $25\%$, shade $25$ out of $100$ squares.

title: Simplify a Ratio
description: Reduce a ratio to an equivalent ratio in simplest form by dividing both terms by a common factor.
condition: A ratio a:b where a and b share a common factor greater than 1.
response: Divide both terms by their greatest common factor.
label: Principle / Model
example: The ratio $10:5$ simplifies by dividing both terms by 5 to get $2:1$.
notes: Used to verify equivalence of ratios.

title: Solve Missing Value Using Constant Rate
description: Use a constant rate relationship (rate = total ÷ time or quantity) to find a missing quantity.
condition: Given total amount and constant rate information with one missing variable.
response: Set up equation using rate × time = total (or equivalent) and solve for the unknown.
label: Principle / Model
example: Rate at job A: $132 \div 6 = 22$. Job B rate: $22 + 4 = 26$. Hours at job B: $182 \div 26 = 7$.
